channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending criminal probe of US woman's death
item.description: Swiss police arrested several people following the death of an unidentified 64-year-old woman from the U.S. Midwest who on Sept. 23 became the first person to use the device.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nibi the 'diva' beaver won't be released, Massachusetts governor decides
item.description: The question of whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi can stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild has been resolved, thanks to the governor of Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Couple reunited after volunteer rescue pilot threatened with arrest in North Carolina
item.description: Jordan Seidhom, who decided to go help those impacted by Helene with his own helicopter on his own dime, was able to rescue Susan Coffey off a mountainside.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, NewsNation

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuelan man uses love for cooking to make a home in Texas
item.description: Saul Aguero, a Venezuelan native who moved across the world to make Austin his home, shares his love for the kitchen with the community.
item.dc:creator: Esmeralda Zamora, NewsNation

item.title: Jets' Aaron Rodgers becomes the 9th NFL player to throw for 60,000 yards
item.description: Aaron Rodgers reached another milestone in his stellar career on Sunday by becoming just the ninth player in NFL history to throw for 60,000 yards in regular-season games.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Helene flooding in NC stirs yellow jacket swarms, prompting distribution of Benadryl and EpiPens
item.description: Swarms of stinging insects in North Carolina are swarming those trying to recover from the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, prompting the distribution of Benadryl and epinephrine injections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters in North Carolina and Georgia have bigger problems than politics. Helene changed everything
item.description: VILAS, N.C. (AP) — Brad Farrington pulls over to grab a case of water bottles being passed out in Vilas, a small rural community tucked away in the Blue Ridge Mountains. He’s on his way to help a friend who lost much of what he owned when Hurricane Helene blew through last weekend.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: For small cities across Alabama with Haitian populations, Springfield is a cautionary tale
item.description: ENTERPRISE, Ala. (AP) — The transition from the bustling Port-au-Prince, Haiti, to a small Alabama city on the southernmost tip of the Appalachian mountain range was challenging for Sarah Jacques. But over the course of a year, the 22-year-old got used to the quiet and settled in.
item.dc:creator: SAFIYAH RIDDLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Affluent Virginia suburb rocked by love triangle murders as prosecutors highlight key evidence
item.description: Virginia prosecutors say blood-splatter analysis led to the arrest of Brendan Banfield in connection with the 2023 murders of his wife and an unknown man.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Verdugo's go-ahead single and acrobatic catch lift Yankees over Royals 6-5 in ALDS opener
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Verdugo hit a tiebreaking single in the seventh inning and saved at least one run with a sliding catch along the left-field line, boosting the New York Yankees over the Kansas City Royals 6-5 on Saturday night in their AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mets use another October rally to score 5 runs in 8th and top Phillies 6-2 in Game 1 of NLDS
item.description: Mark Vientos and Brandon Nimmo keyed another late New York comeback, helping the Mets break through for five runs in the eighth inning against a pair of All-Star relievers as they rallied past the Philadelphia Phillies 6-2 on Saturday in Game 1 of their National League Division Series.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dead whale washes ashore in Queens after being seen floating in New York waters
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A dead whale washed ashore at Fort Tilden in Queens on Saturday morning, according to a Facebook post by the Atlantic Marine Conservation Society. The dead whale was first discovered floating in the waters surrounding New York on Thursday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Frontier flight catches fire while making hard landing in Las Vegas
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Flames and smoke were seen coming from a plane that made a hard landing at Harry Reid International Airport in Las Vegas on Saturday afternoon. Frontier Flight 1326 landed in Las Vegas around 3:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Powerful new explosions rocked Beirut's southern suburbs late Saturday as Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon, also striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Death toll from Hurricane Helene rises to 227 as grim task of recovering bodies continues
item.description: The death toll from Hurricane Helene has inched up to 227 as the grim task of recovering bodies continues more than a week after the monster storm ravaged the U.S. Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Where do Trump, Harris stand on marijuana policy?
item.description: In a poll taken last year, roughly 70% of American adults said marijuana should be legal — but where do the presidential candidates stand?
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Former New York governor and stepson assaulted during evening walk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. David Paterson and his stepson sustained minor injuries when they were assaulted on a Manhattan street by four men and a woman as they went for an evening walk, police said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tropical Storm Milton forms in the Gulf, NHC says
item.description: Tropical Depression Fourteen has strengthened into Tropical Storm Milton, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Keanu Reeves spins out at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in pro auto racing debut
item.description: Hollywood star Keanu Reeves made his professional auto racing debut on Saturday in an event in which “The Matrix” star spun out at famed Indianapolis Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What happens to items surrendered to TSA?
item.description: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) said it does not confiscate items. According to the agency, if someone shows up with a prohibited item, they are given a choice on what to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: False and misleading claims about Hurricane Helene are spreading about the storm and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Utah State doctoral student looks to help her hometown struck by Hurricane Helene
item.description: One Utah State University student is trying to look out for her and her neighbors in North Carolina. Debris from homes and businesses after Hurricane Helene is still being cleaned up almost a week after landfall.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man charged with helping Idaho inmate escape during a hospital ambush sentenced to life in prison
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho man who prosecutors said ambushed and shot correctional officers at a Boise hospital in a plot to help a fellow white supremacist gang member escape from prison was sentenced to life behind bars on Friday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Obama’s first campaign stop for Harris will be in the Pittsburgh area on Oct. 10, according to a senior campaign official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Nigerian track star Oba Femi shines as NXT superstar
item.description: Femi will defend his title Tuesday night on the CW Network. It's a show that also features WWE legend Randy Orton.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, Fox 5

item.title: Advocates urge Ohio to restore voter registrations removed in apparent violation of federal law
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Voter advocacy organizations have put Ohio's Republican elections chief on notice that voters are being systematically removed from the rolls in several counties in apparent violation of federal law.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department finds Georgia is 'deliberately indifferent' to unchecked abuses at its prisons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia prison officials are “deliberately indifferent” to unchecked deadly violence, widespread drug use, extortion and sexual abuse at state lockups, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BY JEFF MARTIN, KEVIN McGILL and ALANNA DURKIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
